steps.title: Explain the resistance
steps.content: The sentence says no justification for raising the toll is likely to persuade some drivers who already believe the current toll is blank. The blank must describe a belief that would make any increase unacceptable.

steps.title: Reject weak objections
steps.content: Choice B means disputed, but a disputed toll might still be justified. Choice C means unclear, which does not explain resistance to an increase. Choice D means justified, the opposite of the needed idea. A belief that the current toll is already unreasonably expensive explains why drivers would reject a higher toll. The answer is A.
